picks.headline: Idaho entry-level with well and power installed
picks.reason: 40 acres with water well and power already in place eliminates the two largest infrastructure barriers. No red flags, solid agricultural suitability for livestock, and $1,375/acre is reasonable for turnkey utilities. Owner financing further reduces barriers.

picks.headline: Montana bargain with year-round creek access
picks.reason: 80 acres at $475/acre with reliable year-round creek, timber, hunting potential, and zero HOA restrictions. Heavily timbered terrain requires clearing before building, but acreage and water security offset this work. Best price-to-land-value ratio.

picks.headline: Minnesota cabin with utilities and year-round water
picks.reason: 87 acres with functioning cabin, year-round well water, electric power, and paved access—immediately liveable without building. Timber harvest and hunting potential add value. No red flags despite remote location; only concern is poor agricultural suitability.

picks.headline: Tennessee creek property with no restrictions
picks.reason: 25 acres with year-round creek, paved access, zero HOA restrictions, and confirmed buildability (surveyed and perc-tested). Highest homestead fit score (77) and mild climate support self-sufficient living, though smaller acreage and $3,560/acre pricing limit scale.

picks.headline: New Mexico ranch at exceptional scale and price
picks.reason: 640 acres at $500/acre with year-round well water, solar/cabin infrastructure, and hunting potential. Extreme isolation (40 miles to town) is the sole red flag—excellent for experienced homesteaders comfortable with genuine remoteness and self-sufficiency.
